source.title: Documented seed: chapter summary with decision cue
source.source_note: This extraction is reconstructed from the documented OpenClaw slide pattern summaries, not from the original slide screenshot.

claim: Close the chapter with one dominant takeaway and one explicit decision implication.
visual_anchor: A dominant summary block supported by a secondary implication block and a signal cue.

composition.structure: One large summary zone, one medium implication zone, and one compact signal or decision object.
composition.alignment_logic: The large and medium blocks share a common grid and baseline while the signal cue aligns to the same frame edge.
composition.asymmetry: The composition stays left-led, but the smaller signal object prevents the page from feeling flat.
composition.image_usage: Optional abstract texture or accent object; the page should remain strong even without a hero image.
highlight_grammar: accent color reserved for the decision cue
strong contrast between main takeaway and secondary implication

components.position_note: Largest block on the left or upper-left
Secondary block aligned inside the same frame
Compact accent object used as a close

why_it_works: The page ends a section with a clear executive takeaway instead of an agenda-style recap.
A visible decision cue turns the summary into an action-oriented close.
Low-density structure gives the main takeaway room to feel important.
anti_patterns: Using three equal summary cards makes the page feel generic.
Overfilling the page with bullets weakens the close.
Removing the decision cue makes the page feel like a leftover note page.

adaptation_notes: Keep the page sparse and decisive.
Use an accent object only to sharpen the close, not to decorate it.
